top_categories.workout_context: 6,000ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Iron Horse Bicycle Classic's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before race day.
Iron Horse Bicycle Classic's paved mountain highway with two major passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 50 miles.
120ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Iron Horse Bicycle Classic, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Iron Horse Bicycle Classic. Train it until it feels boring.
50 miles on paved mountain highway with two major passes burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
6,000ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Iron Horse Bicycle Classic, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved mountain highway with two major passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Base fitness for Iron Horse Bicycle Classic. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Low-cadence force work for 6,000ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Iron Horse Bicycle Classic’s 50 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Iron Horse Bicycle Classic with 6,000ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. Temperature drops of 20-30F between Durango and pass summits.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Iron Horse Bicycle Classic requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on paved mountain highway with two major passes to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 50 miles of paved mountain highway with two major passes in Durango, Colorado.
